name: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3
subtitle: Steel Fabrication | Welding Engineering — AWS D1.1-2020 §2.4, Table 8.8, EN 1993-1-8:2005 §4.5.3

inScope: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Applied Shear Force on Weld Group (V_u / V_Ed)
Applied Normal (Tension/Compression) Force on Weld (N_u / N_Ed)

standards.name: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3
standards.description: Fillet Weld Sizing — Effective Throat, Shear Capacity & Minimum Leg Size per AWS D1.1 / EC3 — Steel Fabrication, Welding Engineering

formulas.expression: a_min_aws_mm = (thicker_part_mm <= 6) ? 3 : (thicker_part_mm <= 12) ? 5 : (thicker_part_mm <= 19) ? 6 : (thicker_part_mm <= 38) ? 8 : 10; a_min_ec3_mm = Math.max(Math.sqrt(thicker_part_mm) - 0.5, 3); a_min_mm = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? a_min_aws_mm : a_min_ec3_mm
formulas.reference: AWS D1.1-2020 Table 8.8 (prequalified minimum weld size); EN 1993-1-8 §4.5.3.1 — a_min = √t_max − 0.5 mm

formulas.expression: F_w_mpa = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? 0.6 * filler_metal_fexx_mpa * 0.707 : (filler_metal_fexx_mpa / Math.sqrt(3)) / 1.25; demand_kn_mm = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(V_demand_kn, 2) + Math.pow(N_demand_kn, 2)) / weld_length_mm; a_req_mm = demand_kn_mm * 1000 / F_w_mpa

formulas.reference: Governing size = maximum of code minimum and calculated requirement; round up to nearest mm
Throat = leg × cos45° = leg × 0.707 — ISO 2553:2019 §3.6; theoretical throat for equal-leg fillet

formulas.expression: F_w_mpa = (weld_standard === 'AWS') ? 0.75 * 0.6 * filler_metal_fexx_mpa : filler_metal_fexx_mpa / (Math.sqrt(3) * 1.25); phi_Rn_kn = F_w_mpa * A_w_mm2 / 1000
V_resultant_kn = Math.sqrt(Math.pow(V_demand_kn, 2) + Math.pow(N_demand_kn, 2)); DCR_weld = V_resultant_kn / phi_Rn_kn
formulas.reference: Resultant force vector check; DCR ≤ 1.0 required — AWS §2.4.1 / EC3 §4.5.3

validation.message: Weld group overstressed — increase leg size, add weld length, or select higher-strength filler metal
Design leg size below code minimum for plate thickness — minimum leg size per AWS Table 8.8 / EC3 §4.5.3 must be met regardless of calculated demand
Weld length < 4× leg size: weld too short relative to size — AWS D1.1 §2.5 minimum effective length requirement violated; increase length or reduce leg size

warnings.message: Weld leg > 75% of plate thickness: excessive heat input risk — risk of burn-through and distortion; maximum weld leg = t − 1.5 mm for plates < 12 mm per AWS §2.5.2
Weld utilisation > 85%: limited reserve for dynamic or fatigue loading — increase leg size by minimum 2 mm or add return welds at ends
Undermatching filler metal: F_EXX < 1.2×F_y of base metal — acceptable for ductility in some cases, but verify per AWS D1.1 Annex I for high-strength steel
Small weld leg under high load: verify welder qualification — leg size < 5 mm requires special quality control per ISO 3834-2
Significant tension component: combined stress state — verify weld orientation; transverse welds to applied tension are 50% stronger than longitudinal per AWS Annex B

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required

legalNotice: ⚠ Engineering decision support only — verify before use. This is a technical simulation, not professional engineering advice.
